title: Clearing Emergency Stop condition
description:
Clearing Emergency Stop condition

Clearing Emergency Stop condition

When the Emergency Stop circuit has been broken, a flag in the controller is set to indicate that the circuit was broken.
  You must clear this flag before you can execute motion commands.  When the flag is set, you will receive errors if you try to turn on motors or move the robot.  The E-STOP LED will be lit on the front panel of the controller.

Follow these steps to clear the emergency stop flag:

1. First, restore the emergency stop circuit.  For example, if you pressed an emergency stop switch, release the switch.

2. Execute a Reset command.  You can execute Reset from the Command Window, a program, or from the Robot Manager Control Panel page.
